passage: The county health department released its annual report on air quality for the metropolitan area, the report found that ozone levels had exceeded federal safety standards on forty-seven days during the previous calendar year. Officials pledged to implement stricter vehicle emission testing.
question: Which choice correctly fixes the sentence boundary error in the first sentence?

choices.A: The county health department released its annual report on air quality for the metropolitan area; the report found that ozone levels had exceeded federal safety standards on forty-seven days during the previous calendar year.
choices.B: The county health department released its annual report on air quality for the metropolitan area the report found that ozone levels had exceeded federal safety standards on forty-seven days during the previous calendar year.
choices.C: The county health department released its annual report on air quality for the metropolitan area: the report found that ozone levels had exceeded federal safety standards on forty-seven days.
choices.D: No change; the comma after 'metropolitan area' is correct because the second clause explains the contents of the report.

explanation: The first sentence is a comma splice—two independent clauses joined by only a comma. 'The county health department released its annual report on air quality for the metropolitan area' is a complete independent clause, and 'the report found that ozone levels had exceeded federal safety standards on forty-seven days during the previous calendar year' is a second complete independent clause. Choice A correctly uses a semicolon to join them. Choice B creates a fused sentence. Choice C uses a colon—less appropriate here because the second clause describes what the report found rather than defining or listing the 'annual report' introduced in the first clause. Choice D incorrectly defends the comma splice.
